授業目的: - Understanding historical backgrounds to contemporary international relations in East Asia- Situating Japan’s diplomatic strategies in global politics
到達目標:
-
Debating on timely problems and recent news related to international relations
- Reading academic and press materials in English 
- Developing critical thinking through oral (debates) and written (essays)
expressions
授業方法: Each class will start with a 15-20 minute discussion based on the weekly reading assignments. Students are expected to read the materials, prepare at least 3 questions or comments on these texts, and actively participate in the discussion. The rest of the session will be a lecture dedicated to different topics every week. The use of generative AI is strictly prohibited.

第1回:
【Startup Week】"Face-to-face class" /《90minutes》
Session 1. Introduction and Orientation. 
- What are the key concepts in the field of IR?
- How were IR shaped in the 19th-21st century’s global history?
- How did they impact Japan?
- What are the key questions of contemporary Japan’s international relations?
Reading of the handed materials and preparation of at least 3 questions or comments on these texts for the next session.
第2回:
【Core Weeks①】 "Face-to-face class" /《90minutes》
Session 2. From Edo to Meiji: Opening and modernising Japan (1600s-1890s).
- Ancient Chinese sphere of influence
- Closing of the country and limited exchanges during Edo
- Following the Opium Wars
- Perry’s black ships and the Meiji restoration
- The modernisation of law, economics, technology and the army
Reading of the handed materials and preparation of at least 3 questions or comments on these texts for the next session.
第3回:
【Core Weeks②】 "Face-to-face class" /《90minutes》
Session 3. Japan’s ascension on the international stage (1890s-1930s)
- International recognition after the Sino-Japanese and Sino-Russian wars
- Intervention in the First World War, disappointment of the Paris conference
- Implication in interwar international cooperation
Reading of the handed materials and preparation of at least 3 questions or comments on these texts for the next session.
第4回:
【Core Weeks③】 "Face-to-face class" /《90minutes》
Session 4. Japanese Imperialism (1890s-1945)
- Extension of the Japanese territory (Hokkaido, Ryukyu)
- Colonisation of Taiwan and the Korean peninsula
- Regional leader against white hegemony, elaboration of Pan-Asianism
- Assimilation policies and violence
Reading of the handed materials and preparation of at least 3 questions or comments on these texts for the next session.
第5回:
【Flex Hours】"Online class（On-demand format）" /《90minutes》
Session 5. Militarism and the Asia-Pacific War (1931-1945)
- Invasion of Manchuria
- Militarisation and alliance with the Axis
- Full-scale war against China and the US
- Japan's war crimes
- Defeat and domestic traumatisms (Hiroshima, Nagasaki)
Reading of the handed materials and preparation of at least 3 questions or comments on these texts for the next session.
第6回:
【Core Weeks④】 "Face-to-face class" /《90minutes》
Session 6 The US Occupation and the San Francisco System (1945-1952/1972)
- A new constitution imposing pacifism
- The 1952 San Francisco Treaties paradox
- American bases on the Japanese territory
- Implications in long-term security policies: the creation of Self-Defense Forces
Reading of the handed materials and preparation of at least 3 questions or comments on these texts for the next session.
第7回:
【Core Weeks⑤】 "Face-to-face class" /《90minutes》
Session 7 Growing Influence as an Economic Leader (1960s-1990s)
- Economic growth and global trade
- Foreign investments with the West and financial participation in global institutions
- Early investments in Asia (Japan’s aid)
- The 1990s economic crisis
Reading of the handed materials and preparation of at least 3 questions or comments on these texts for the next session.
第8回:
【Core Weeks⑥】 "Face-to-face class" /《90minutes》
Session 8 Tensions with the neighbours: Territorial Disputes (1950s-2020s)
- Takeshima/Dokdo with Korea
- Northern territories/Kuril islands with Russia 
- Senkaku/Diaoyu, and South East China Sea islands with China 
- Economic and security implications

MID-TERM QUIZ (30 minutes)
Reading of the handed materials and preparation of at least 3 questions or comments on these texts for the next session.
第9回:
【Core Weeks⑦】 "Face-to-face class" /《90minutes》
Session 9 Tensions with the neighbours: War Memory Issues (1980s- 2020s)
- The Tokyo trial and its controversies
- The rise of anti-Japanism in South Korea and China
- Yasukuni shrine scandals
- Demands for compensation for Comfort women and victims of forced labour 
- History, Geography, and civic education manuals
Reading of the handed materials and preparation of at least 3 questions or comments on these texts for the next session.
第10回:
【Core Weeks⑧】"Face-to-face class" /《90minutes》
Session 10 Security questions from the Cold War
- The retrocession of Okinawa (1972) and US bases on the Japanese territory
- The danger of North Korea
- The Chinese threat: China’s increased military power, maritime law infringements, the Taiwanese question
- Recent cooperation with the US, South Korean, and European armies. 
- US pressures for the rearmament of Japan and the evolution of the Self-Defence Forces
Reading of the handed materials and preparation of at least 3 questions or comments on these texts for the next session.
第11回:
【Core Weeks⑨】"Face-to-face class" /《90minutes》
Session 11. New economic and diplomatic implications with the Global South
- Japan’s aid in Southeast Asia and implications in the ASEAN
- New horizons on the African and South American continents
- Japan’s ‘Indo-Pacific cooperation’: a strategy to constrain China’s Belt and Road? 
- Japan's impulse in the CMTPP negotiations
Reading of the handed materials and preparation of at least 3 questions or comments on these texts for the next session.
第12回:
【Core Weeks⑩】"Face-to-face class" /《90minutes》
Session 12. Idealism as Soft Power and Japan’s weight in international institutions
- Environmental Diplomacy
- Promoting ‘Democracy and Peace’
- Implication in the United Nations
- Role in the G7 and G20
Reading of the handed materials and preparation of at least 3 questions or comments on these texts for the next session.
第13回:
【Core Weeks⑪】  "Face-to-face class" /《90minutes》
Session 13, Cultural Diplomacy and the development of tourism
- The use of sports in diplomacy
- Pop culture boom and the creation of a ‘Cool Japan’
- Development of tourism and hosting of international cultural events
- Impact on domestic politics: a (global) nationalist backlash?
No assignment (preparation for the final exam).
第14回:
【Core Weeks⑫】"Face-to-face class" /《90minutes》

FINAL EXAM
第15回:
【Classes and examinations period】"Face-to-face class" /《90minutes》
Class review, feedback on the final exam.
トピック.授業外学習2: The use of generative AI is strictly prohibited.

参考書/Reference books1: The diplomatic history of postwar Japan
参考書/Reference books2: Pekkanen, Robert J. and Pekkanen, Sadia M.
The Oxford Handbook of Japanese Politics
参考書/Reference books3: Asia Rising: A Handbook of History and International Relations in East, South and Southeast Asia.
課題等へのフィードバック方法Methods for Providing Feed: In-class review (discussions), email, Zoom meetings (if necessary)
成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
成績評価Grading1: Mid-term in class quiz: short and long questions. Final in class examination: quiz questions and short essay.
成績評価Grading2: 授業への参加度（自発性、積極性、主体性、等）／In-class participation,contribution (09)
Active participation in class discussions based on each week's mandatory reading assignment.

name: 【320】International Relations of Japan／International Relations of Japan [EN]
担当者: ラモス　マノン（RAMOS MANON）
